잠언 6:1.content: My son, if you have become collateral for your neighbor, if you have struck your hands in pledge for a stranger,

잠언 6:2.content: you are trapped by the words of your mouth; you are ensnared with the words of your mouth.

잠언 6:3.content: Do this now, my son, and deliver yourself, since you have come into the hand of your neighbor. Go, humble yourself. Press your plea with your neighbor.

잠언 6:4.content: Give no sleep to your eyes, nor slumber to your eyelids.

잠언 6:5.content: Free yourself, like a gazelle from the hand of the hunter, like a bird from the snare of the fowler.

잠언 6:6.content: Go to the ant, you sluggard. Consider her ways, and be wise;

잠언 6:7.content: which having no chief, overseer, or ruler,

잠언 6:8.content: provides her bread in the summer, and gathers her food in the harvest.

잠언 6:9.content: How long will you sleep, sluggard? When will you arise out of your sleep?

잠언 6:10.content: A little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:

잠언 6:11.content: so your poverty will come as a robber, and your scarcity as an armed man.

잠언 6:12.content: A worthless person, a man of iniquity, is he who walks with a perverse mouth,

잠언 6:13.content: who winks with his eyes, who signals with his feet, who motions with his fingers,

잠언 6:14.content: in whose heart is perverseness, who devises evil continually, who always sows discord.

잠언 6:15.content: Therefore his calamity will come suddenly. He will be broken suddenly, and that without remedy.

잠언 6:16.content: There are six things which Yahweh hates; yes, seven which are an abomination to him:

잠언 6:17.content: arrogant eyes, a lying tongue, hands that shed innocent blood,

잠언 6:18.content: a heart that devises wicked schemes, feet that are swift in running to mischief,

잠언 6:19.content: a false witness who utters lies, and he who sows discord among brothers.

잠언 6:20.content: My son, keep your father’s commandment, and don’t forsake your mother’s teaching.

잠언 6:21.content: Bind them continually on your heart. Tie them around your neck.

잠언 6:22.content: When you walk, it will lead you. When you sleep, it will watch over you. When you awake, it will talk with you.

잠언 6:23.content: For the commandment is a lamp, and the law is light. Reproofs of instruction are the way of life,

잠언 6:24.content: to keep you from the immoral woman, from the flattery of the wayward wife’s tongue.

잠언 6:25.content: Don’t lust after her beauty in your heart, neither let her captivate you with her eyelids.

잠언 6:26.content: For a prostitute reduces you to a piece of bread. The adulteress hunts for your precious life.

잠언 6:27.content: Can a man scoop fire into his lap, and his clothes not be burned?

잠언 6:28.content: Or can one walk on hot coals, and his feet not be scorched?

잠언 6:29.content: So is he who goes in to his neighbor’s wife. Whoever touches her will not be unpunished.

잠언 6:30.content: Men don’t despise a thief if he steals to satisfy himself when he is hungry;

잠언 6:31.content: but if he is found, he shall restore seven times. He shall give all the wealth of his house.

잠언 6:32.content: He who commits adultery with a woman is void of understanding. He who does it destroys his own soul.

잠언 6:33.content: He will get wounds and dishonor. His reproach will not be wiped away.

잠언 6:34.content: For jealousy arouses the fury of the husband. He won’t spare in the day of vengeance.

잠언 6:35.content: He won’t regard any ransom, neither will he rest content, though you give many gifts.
